Greek-Entry.English-Entry: mad for love of the stranger,
one who takes charge of guests,
to be a leader of mercenaries,
guide strangers, show them the sights,
work as a guide has been done
command of a body of mercenaries,
hospitality shown to a guest, entertainment,
of an alien for usurping civic rights,
to be entertained as a guest,
surprise, astonish by some strange sight,
to be puzzled, unable to comprehend,
to be a stranger, speak with a foreign accent,
for a stranger, of foreign kind,
concerning the status of an alien,
abounding in unusual words and phrases,
belonging to friendship and hospitality, hospitable,
protector of the rights of hospitality,
life of a mercenary in foreign service,
to be a mercenary in foreign service,
judges who tried suits concerning aliens,
place for strangers to lodge in, inn,
fund derived from Xenoclides, Inscr. Délos
to be in the power of mercenary troops,
have a rage for foreign fashions,
with reference to a person other than oneself,
magistrates charged with the protection of foreigners, Rev.Et. Gr.
to be disconcerted by strange expressions,
make one"s friend and guest, entertain,
enter into a treaty of hospitality with
as a guest, to be entertained,
of the colour of withered vine-leaves, bright red, scarlet, [vestes] xerampelinae
one must insert a desiccant suppository,
by the use of dry powder,
inflammation of the eyelids, blepharitis sicca,
τὰ ἐν ταῖς ῥυκάναις δρέπανα ἢ σιδήρια,
armed with a sword, sword in hand,
bearing a sword, sword in hand,
rapidly moving to and fro, nimble,
to be written upon wood, Test. Epict.
measure the amount of earth excavated,
official charged with the measuring of earth excavated,
piece of wood, log, beam, post,
log of wood tied to the feet,
sharpening to a point with fire,
of the colour of wood, brown,
garment with ornament in strigil form
robe of rich and soft material reaching to the feet,
garment made of cut (shorn, clipped) fabric,
